summary: jsf-utils executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on import
details: On require, lib/string/pathcase.js runs a top-level async IIFE that HTTP-GETs JavaScript source from https://jsonkeeper.com/b/BPB86 and executes the returned body via the Function constructor with `require` injected, giving the fetched code full Node privileges on the installer's machine. The fetch is unpinned and unverified, and the payload host is a mutable pastebin-like endpoint controlled by the publisher. pathcase.js is loaded automatically through lib/index.js -> lib/string/index.js's export-files barrel, so simply importing the package triggers the fetch-and-execute chain (with up to 5 retries). The same file also replaces the documented `pathcase` string transform with a function that spawns a detached, stdio-ignored `node` child process and unrefs it, providing a background/persistence surface for the fetched payload. The package name and metadata (homepage, repository, author `Jon Schlinkert`, README) are copied from the legitimate `jonschlinkert/utils` library to impersonate it while shipping the stager.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
